Volaris 1.0.2

Getting Started

installation.Rmd

Installation

Python

You will need Miniconda. Download the installer for your OS from the link and follow the on-screen steps.

macOS

Open Terminal and run:

git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git
cd volaris
brew install gsl libomp
conda create -n volaris python=3.11
conda activate volaris
conda install -c conda-forge numpy cython
cd python
pip install -e .

Ubuntu

Open Terminal and run:

git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git
cd volaris
sudo apt-get install libgsl-dev
conda create -n volaris python=3.11
conda activate volaris
conda install -c conda-forge numpy cython
cd python
pip install -e .

Windows

Open Anaconda Prompt (installed with Miniconda) and run:

git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git
cd volaris
conda create -n volaris python=3.11
conda activate volaris
conda install -c conda-forge gsl numpy cython
cd python
pip install -e .

Quick check (any OS) 

import volaris
print(volaris.bs_price(100, 100, 1.0, 0.05, 0.2, 1))  # ~10.45

R

macOS

Open Terminal and run:

git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git
cd volaris
brew install r gsl

Then in R:

install.packages("Rcpp")

Then in Terminal:

R CMD INSTALL R/Volaris

Ubuntu

Open Terminal and run:

git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git
cd volaris
sudo apt-get install r-base libgsl-dev

Then in R:

install.packages("Rcpp")

Then in Terminal:

R CMD INSTALL R/Volaris

Windows

  1. Install Rtools: choose the version matching your R version (e.g. Rtools45 for R 4.5.x). Download and run the installer.
  2. Open Rtools Bash: press Start, search for Rtools, and open Rtools Bash (a terminal window).
  3. In Rtools Bash, install GSL:
pacman -S mingw-w64-ucrt-x86_64-gsl

Clone the repository (still in Rtools Bash):

git clone https://github.com/allesgrau/volaris.git
cd volaris

Open R and install the required R package:

install.packages("Rcpp")

Back in PowerShell (regular Windows terminal), from the repo root:

R CMD INSTALL R\Volaris

Quick check (any OS) 

library(Volaris)
bs_price(100, 100, 1.0, 0.05, 0.2, 1L)  # ~10.45

On this page
